question: Which operation can be performed in Camunda Operate's process-instance modification feature?

options.text: Add a new token to any flow node in the diagram.
Edit the BPMN model and redeploy it from within Operate.
Roll a completed instance back to a previous state.
Change the process definition version of a running instance in place.

a.text: Process-instance modification in Operate lets an operator add a token to any flow node, cancel a specific token, or set variables on a running instance.

b.text: BPMN editing is in Modeler, not Operate.

c.text: Operate cannot roll back a completed instance; history is immutable.

d.text: Changing the process definition version is a separate Operate feature called migration, not modification.

explanation: Operate's process-instance modification adds and cancels tokens and updates variables on a running instance. Editing the BPMN model is a Modeler task, migration (changing a definition version) is a separate Operate feature, and history is immutable.
